meta.purpose: Config + data for the basic role-play generator. Three parts: (A) situation maker, (B) audio listener & grader, (C) question generator. Performance indicators (PIs) are the scoring criteria — the generator holds them constant per event and randomizes the situation around them.
meta.applies_to: DECA role-plays (Team Decision Making + Series events) and FBLA hybrid/role-play events.

output_contract.scenario: { role, company, situation, your_task, judge_role, performance_indicators:[...], prep_minutes }
output_contract.grade: { toolId:'roleplay', results:[ {criterion(=PI), band, points, justification, fix} ], delivery:{...if audio}, overall, meta:{transcript} }

context_banks.comment: The situation maker draws ONE value from each bank (plus temperature + recent-history exclusion) to make every scenario unique while the PIs stay fixed.
twists: the customer is unhappy and considering leaving
leadership wants a recommendation by tomorrow
two options are on the table and you must pick one
the budget was just cut in half
a mistake was made that must be addressed

scoring_form.type: DECA role-play evaluation form (adapt totals for FBLA hybrids to their rating sheet)
scoring_form.structure: Each performance indicator is scored in one of four bands; the PI scores plus a 21st-Century-Skills block and an overall-impression score sum to 100.
twenty_first_century_skills: Reason and make judgments / think critically
Overall impression and response to the judge's questions
scoring_form.note: DECA publishes the exact PIs and per-PI point value per event; pull the official list per event and drop them in as the criteria.

example_events.event: Principles of Marketing (DECA, individual, Business Admin Core cluster)
performance_indicators: Explain the nature of positive customer relations
Identify the elements of the promotional mix
Explain the concept of marketing strategies

example_events.event: Hospitality & Tourism Team Decision Making (DECA, team of 2)
performance_indicators: Explain the nature of quality management
Explain the nature of marketing planning
